texto: When I was first pitched the show, I was like, 'Oh, God, a soapy show called 'Mistresses.'' But it wasn't that at all. It wasn't a bunch of women pulling guns on each other and stabbing each other in the back. We really have a core friendship.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Jes Macallan: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a amizade. O trecho “When I was first pitched the show, I was like, 'Oh, God, a soapy show called 'Mistresses.'' But i…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que amizade, women, god pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de termino e god.

frase_original: When I was first pitched the show, I was like, 'Oh, God, a soapy show called 'Mistresses.'' But it wasn't that at all. It wasn't a bunch of women pulling guns on each other and stabbing each other in the back. We really have a core friendship.
